howItWorks.intro: This christmas budget calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Per Adult = Total Gift Budget / (Adults + Kids x 1.5)
Per Child = Per Adult x 1.5 (weighted higher)
Grand Total = Gifts + Food + Decor + Travel
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much does the average American spend on Christmas?
faq.answer: The average American spends about $920 on holiday gifts, according to Gallup. Total holiday spending including food, travel, and decorations averages $1,500-2,000 per household.

faq.question: How should I allocate my Christmas budget?
faq.answer: A common rule is 50% gifts, 25% food/hosting, 15% travel, 10% decorations. Spend more on kids than adults (1.5-2x). Set a per-person limit and stick to it to avoid overspending.

faq.question: How can I avoid holiday debt?
faq.answer: Start a Christmas savings fund in January (divide total budget by 11 months). Use cash or debit only. Set spending limits with family members. Consider gift exchanges or Secret Santa to reduce the number of gifts.

faq.question: When should I start Christmas shopping?
faq.answer: Start in October for the best selection without holiday markups. Black Friday and Cyber Monday offer genuine deals on electronics and toys. After-Christmas sales (Dec 26+) are great for wrapping paper and decorations for next year.

sections.title: What Is the Christmas Budget Calculator?
sections.body: The Christmas Budget Calculator helps you plan your spending with confidence so you stay on budget without cutting corners. Instead of guessing or spending hours on manual calculations, get accurate results in seconds. Enter your details above and let the calculator do the work.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Budgeting decisions are easier when you can see the complete picture before committing to spending. This calculator breaks down the real costs so you can allocate your money with confidence and avoid overspending in areas that matter less to you.
